explanation: The property states that globally, if the self-test fails (selfTestFail), then the system must not be in the ventilating state (!ventilating). This ensures that the controller is prevented from proceeding to ventilation after a self-test failure.

The property states that globally, if 'selfTestFail' (the SelfTest fails) becomes true, then 'ventilating' (controller proceeds to ventilation) must always remain false from that point onwards. This ensures that if the SelfTest fails, the system is prevented from entering the ventilation state at any future time.

The property states that globally (G), if the self-test fails (bool_exp2), then it must always be the case (G) that ventilation is not active (!bool_exp3). This ensures that if the self-test fails, the system will never proceed to ventilation.

The property states that globally, if the SelfTest fails, then it must always be the case that the system is not ventilating. This directly captures the requirement that after a SelfTest failure, the controller shall not be able to proceed to ventilation, ensuring that ventilation is permanently prevented.

The property states that globally (G), if the self-test fails (bool_exp2), then it must always be the case (G) that the system is not ventilating (negation of bool_exp3). This ensures that after a self-test failure, the controller never enters a ventilating state.

The property states that globally, if the SelfTest fails (selfTestFail), then it must always be true that the system is not ventilating (G (! ventilating)). This accurately captures the requirement that once the self-test fails, the controller shall not be able to proceed to ventilation at any future point.

The LTL property states that globally, if the SelfTest fails (represented by 'selfTestFail'), then the system shall never proceed to ventilation (represented by '!ventilating') from that point onwards. This ensures that the controller does not allow ventilation if the self-test has detected a fault, adhering to the safety requirement.

The LTL property states that globally (G), if the self-test fails (selfTestFail), then it must always be the case from that point onwards (G) that ventilation is not active (!ventilating). This captures the requirement that once the self-test fails, the controller cannot proceed to ventilation.

The property states that if the self-test fails, the controller must not be able to proceed to ventilation. This is translated as a global implication: if 'selfTestFail' occurs at any point, then 'ventilating' must never become true from that point onward.

The property states that globally, whenever the system is in 'SelfTest Mode' and the 'SelfTest fails', the controller should not be in the 'ventilating' state. This ensures that the system is blocked from proceeding to ventilation as long as the self-test failure condition is present during self-test mode.
